---
title: Factor influence on identification
---

```{python}
#| code-fold: true
#| echo: false
%load_ext autoreload
%autoreload 2

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsClassifier
import sklearn.neural_network
from sklearn.svm import SVC
import sklearn.tree
from sklearn.model_selection import cross_validate, StratifiedShuffleSplit
from sklearn.metrics import (
    make_scorer, 
    accuracy_score, 
    precision_score, 
    recall_score, 
    f1_score, 
    cohen_kappa_score
)

from violin_identification.loader import load_recordings, filter_robust_cohort
from violin_identification.features import get_features_and_labels
from core import Recording, LTAS, LTCC, MFCC

recordings = load_recordings()
recordings = filter_robust_cohort(recordings)

import pandas as pd
from sklearn.base import clone
from sklearn.preprocessing import LabelEncoder
```

## Methods
Train a baseline classifier on different excerpts/players. Test it on same excerpt/player (but different takes) and different excerpts/players (generalization score).
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| output: asis

import pandas as pd
import inspect
from IPython.display import Markdown, display

from violin_identification.experiments.influence_excerpts import (
    run_one_factor_evaluation,
)

source_code = inspect.getsource(run_one_factor_evaluation)

markdown_block = f"```python\n{source_code}\n```"
display(Markdown(markdown_block))
```


## Results 
```{python}
def plot_excerpt_breakdown(results_df, metric="kappa", save_path=None):
    """
    Plots the generalization gap excerpt-by-excerpt for a single classifier,
    sorted from best to worst generalization performance.
    """
    df_plot = results_df.copy()

    # Sort
    gen_data = df_plot[df_plot["test_type"] == "Different Excerpt"]
    excerpt_scores = gen_data.groupby("excerpt_trained_on")[metric].mean()
    df_plot["gen_score"] = df_plot["excerpt_trained_on"].map(excerpt_scores)
    df_plot.sort_values(
        by=["dataset", "gen_score"], ascending=[True, True], inplace=True
    )

    sns.set_theme(style="whitegrid", context="paper", font_scale=1.1)

    g = sns.catplot(
        data=df_plot,
        x="excerpt_trained_on",
        y=metric,
        hue="test_type",
        # col="feature",
        row="dataset",
        kind="box",
        sharex=False,
        height=4.5,
        aspect=1.4,
        palette=["#4C72B0", "#C44E52"],
        boxprops={"alpha": 0.85},
        linewidth=1.2,
    )

    g.set_axis_labels(
        "Training Excerpt (Sorted by Best Generalization)",
        metric.replace("_", " ").title(),
    )
    g.set_titles(
        row_template="Dataset: {row_name}",
        col_template="Feature: {col_name}",
        size=12,
        weight="bold",
    )

    for ax in g.axes.flat:
        for label in ax.get_xticklabels():
            label.set_rotation(45)
            label.set_ha("right")

        ax.set_ylim(bottom=min(-0.1, ax.get_ylim()[0]), top=1.05)
        ax.axhline(1.0, color="black", linewidth=0.5, linestyle="--")

    sns.move_legend(
        g,
        "lower center",
        bbox_to_anchor=(0.5, -0.08),
        ncol=2,
        title="Evaluation Context",
        frameon=True,
    )

    g.figure.suptitle(
        "Excerpt-by-Excerpt Generalization Gap", y=1.02, fontsize=16, weight="bold"
    )

    plt.tight_layout()

    if save_path:
        plt.savefig(save_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")
        print(f"Plot saved to {save_path}")

    plt.show()
```

```{python}
def plot_generalization_gap(results_df, split_column, metric="accuracy"):
    df_plot = results_df.copy()

    # Dynamically define column and test names based on the split variable
    target_col = f"{split_column}_trained_on"
    gen_test_name = f"Different {split_column.capitalize()}"

    # Sort by generalization score
    gen_data = df_plot[df_plot["test_type"] == gen_test_name]
    scores = gen_data.groupby(target_col)[metric].mean()
    df_plot["gen_score"] = df_plot[target_col].map(scores)
    df_plot.sort_values(by=["dataset", "gen_score"], inplace=True)

    # Minimal Boxplot
    g = sns.catplot(
        data=df_plot,
        x=target_col,
        y=metric,
        hue="test_type",
        row="dataset",
        kind="box",
        sharex=False,
        height=4,
        aspect=1.5,
    )

    # Rotate labels for readability
    for ax in g.axes.flat:
        for label in ax.get_xticklabels():
            label.set_rotation(45)
            label.set_ha("right")

    plt.show()
```

### Excerpts

```{python}
results_excerpts = pd.read_csv("../data/interim/excerpts_results.csv")
plot_generalization_gap(results_excerpts, split_column="excerpt", metric="accuracy")
```

### Players
```{python}
results_players = pd.read_csv("../data/interim/players_results.csv")
plot_generalization_gap(results_players, split_column="player", metric="accuracy")
```
